name: Database Session Manager
description: Provides async SQLAlchemy database session management for the backend API. Configures a global async engine from settings and exposes a dependency injection function (get_db) that yields database sessions with automatic cleanup.

types.name: AsyncSession
types.description: SQLAlchemy async database session (imported from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio)

functions.name: get_db
functions.description: Async generator that yields a database session for dependency injection. Creates a new AsyncSession from the global async_sessionmaker, yields it for use, and automatically handles cleanup on exit. Designed for use with FastAPI Depends().

error_cases.name: DatabaseConnectionError
error_cases.condition: If database connection cannot be established (invalid URL, network failure, authentication failure)

error_cases.name: SessionCreationError
error_cases.condition: If async_sessionmaker fails to create session

preconditions: Global 'engine' must be initialized with valid database_url from settings
postconditions: Session is automatically closed after context manager exits
No commit happens automatically (expire_on_commit=False is set)
side_effects: Automatically closes database session on context exit

structured_side_effects.description: Acquires and releases connection from engine pool

invariants: Global 'engine' is created once at module import with echo=False
Global 'async_session' sessionmaker is configured with expire_on_commit=False
Database URL comes from settings.database_url and never changes after initialization
Each call to get_db() creates a fresh session instance
requires: settings.database_url must be a valid async database URL (e.g., postgresql+asyncpg://...)

data_access.rationale: Reads database configuration from settings to initialize engine. Does not write to data tier directly - individual sessions returned by get_db() are used by consumers to read/write.

side_effects.rationale: Manages database connection lifecycle via SQLAlchemy engine and session pool
